title: xyris piraquarae
intro: Xyris piraquarae is a species of flowering plant in the family Xyridaceae, commonly known as a type of yellow-eyed grass or pygmy iris. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. Xyris piraquarae has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, aiding in wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and rhizomes are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
